universality_class.definition: effectively describable systems that maintain distinguishable commitments, permit constrained commitment or state change, preserve grounds or dependencies relevant to revision, and support finite recovery queries over histories

requires: uniform finite recovery of registered distinctions

representation_family.definition: finite or recursively presented encodings with effective construction and one uniform terminating recovery procedure for each registered finite query

bounded_theorem: For every system in C-EFFECTIVE-REASONING-001 and every P-RCCD-FAITHFUL-001 faithful representation in E-EFFECTIVE-REP-001, the representation must encode distinctions equivalent to RCCD components R1-R5. Every successful materially distinct registered architecture is bidirectionally reconstructible with RCCD plus domain-specific parameters and no additional necessary kernel commitment. Therefore RCCD is a necessary nontrivial common kernel for C relative to P and E.

supporting_findings.common_factor: RCCD extracted without selecting a scalar winner
supporting_findings.cross_feature_composition: all registered six-feature conjunction preserved on the frozen effectively presented subclass
supporting_findings.representation_invariance: twelve broader representation transformations preserved RCCD commitments
supporting_findings.alternative_basis_search: no strictly cheaper non-equivalent reconstruction found in the expanded registered search
supporting_findings.lower_bounds: each R1-R5 component has a paired-countermodel conditional lower bound
supporting_findings.frontier: RCCD FARA LTS-PROV and COALG-DYN form one commitment-equivalence class with three Pareto-incomparable realizations

excluded_claims: unrestricted universality over every possible form of reasoning
uniqueness outside the defined C P E scope
actual brains language models courts scientists or other systems internally implement RCCD
external_requirements: held-out cross-context studies with frozen mappings
empirical bridge studies using discriminating interventions

post_internal_stopping_rule.internal_exploration: stop adding broad supportive mappings as a substitute for falsification
reopen_conditions: a valid counterexample inside C violates the theorem
an alternative basis preserves P within E while omitting an RCCD-equivalent commitment
a held-out context requires a genuine new primitive rather than a domain parameter or derived adapter
external review identifies circularity unfair machinery accounting or an invalid lower bound
post_internal_stopping_rule.next_phase: external review replication held-out validation and empirical correspondence

internal_answer: Yes. Relative to the explicitly defined class C, preservation contract P, and effective representation family E, RCCD is a universal structural kernel. An unrestricted claim about all possible reasoning remains unsupported.
